threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you ask for directions to the nearest tube and everyone just stares, then points you toward the bus stop.
Spotted a guy on the 137 who spent the whole ride trying to convince a pigeon to sit on his shoulder. Only in Clapham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of kids turning an abandoned lot into a makeshift skate park, laughing like they'd just won the lottery.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can start your day with a coffee from a 24-hour Späti, then stumble into an all-night club before breakfast. Only in Kreuzberg, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction when a bus is late; they’re not surprised. Out-of-towners act like it’s a personal offense.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a whiff of fresh pastries while waiting for the 5 at UN Plaza, only to watch it zoom past. Classic Muni moment—better luck next time!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat paella; in Gràcia, you'll find way more kebab shops than traditional places. It's all about the late-night bites.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their eager faces at the Mercat de la Llibertat—trying to haggle over prices of everything, even the fruit.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the Overground is a novelty. Locals know it's just another option for the delays, not a sightseeing tour.
Heard a guy on the 47 last night passionately arguing with a pigeon about which way to go. The pigeon just blinked, unfazed. Classic Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a grandma at the Kottbusser Tor U-Bahn platform handing out homemade muffins. That’s when I felt the real spirit of this place.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the BVG drivers greet you like old friends? It's a tiny touch, but in Berlin's bustle, it feels oddly personal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their reaction when a bus driver stops for a dog on the tracks—eye rolls and a resigned sigh instead of outrage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 with a mime performing his act while we waited at the stop. Only in SF do you get free entertainment while stuck in transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas. They miss the tiny bakeries tucked away on side streets, where locals grab their morning calentitos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they stand on the wrong side of the escalator—always blocking traffic like it’s their first day navigating the metro.
